headline: CarMax (KMX) Q2 Beat Came With A Warning On Costs And Execution
summary: CarMax reported Q2 results that topped Wall Street expectations. Management highlighted operational inefficiencies and cost pressures weighing on performance. The company plans to refine retail pricing, vehicle selection, logistics, and its digital customer experience. CarMax, traded as NYSE:KMX, is drawing attention after Q2 results outpaced forecasts while management pointed to friction in the business. The stock last closed at $52.9, with the share price up 30.2% over the past month and...
